originalTitle: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DR Congo a global health emergency
title: Africa's Big Team Helps Stop Sickness in DR Congo!
content:
Hello, My Amazing African Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly news reporter for Africa, ready to tell you about something important. We're going to talk about how big helpers are working together to keep people safe. It’s a story about strength and working as a team!

A Sickness Called Ebola

Sometimes, people can get sick. Most times, it's just a small cough or a tummy ache that gets better quickly. But there is a sickness called Ebola. It's a special kind of sickness that can make people very, very unwell. It's like a very bad flu, but much more serious.

Where is This Happening?

This sickness has been spreading in a place called the Democratic Republic of Congo. We often call it DR Congo for short. DR Congo is a beautiful country right here in Africa, just like where you live! It has amazing rivers, forests, and lots of children just like you. Did you know? Africa is a giant continent! It has 54 different countries, all with wonderful people and stories. DR Congo is one of those special countries.

What is an 'Outbreak'?

When we say there is an "outbreak," it means that a sickness has started to spread in one area. Imagine if a few kids in your class got the sniffles, and then a few more. That would be a small outbreak of sniffles in your class! The Ebola outbreak means that this special sickness started to spread in some towns in DR Congo.

Who Are the Big Helpers?

There's a very important group of people called the World Health Organization. We call them WHO for short. Can you say WHO? Good job! WHO is like a super-duper doctor team for the whole wide world. Their job is to help countries stay healthy and stop sickness from spreading. They are like the world's health superheroes!

What Does 'Global Health Emergency' Mean?

Now, the WHO team said something special about the Ebola sickness in DR Congo. They said it's a "global health emergency." This sounds a bit scary, but let me explain! It means that this sickness is very serious. It means it needs special attention and help from everyone, all around the world. Think of it like this: If your friend fell and really hurt their knee, you would get a grown-up to help, right? And maybe other grown-ups would help too! A global health emergency means lots of grown-ups and special helpers from many countries are coming together to help the people in DR Congo. It's like a big, urgent call for help!

Is it Everywhere? Is it a 'Pandemic'?

You might hear big words like "pandemic." A "pandemic" means a sickness has spread almost everywhere in the whole world. It means lots and lots of people in many, many countries are getting sick. But listen closely: the sickness in DR Congo is *not* a pandemic. It is only in certain places in DR Congo. The WHO team said it needs urgent help, but it has *not* spread all over the world. This is really good news! It means that the helpers are working super hard to keep it in one place and stop it from spreading further.

How Does Ebola Spread?

Ebola is not like a cough or a sneeze that can fly through the air to reach you from far away. It only spreads when someone touches the blood or other body fluids of a very sick person. It means you have to be very, very close to someone who is already very sick. This is why doctors and nurses who help sick people wear special clothes and gloves. They know how to keep themselves safe while they help others. So, most people, especially children like you, are very safe.

What Are the Grown-ups Doing to Help?

Lots of amazing people are working hard! * **Doctors and Nurses:** They are taking care of people who are sick, giving them special medicines to help them feel better. * **Scientists:** They are working to understand the sickness better and find even more ways to stop it. They are like super-sleuths for health! * **Community Workers:** These are brave people from the local towns who are teaching everyone how to stay safe. They tell people about washing hands and how to get help if they feel sick. * **The WHO Team:** They are helping to send doctors, medicines, and supplies to DR Congo. They are making sure everyone works together like a big team. Did you know? Doctors and nurses are real-life heroes! They work so hard to make sure everyone is healthy and happy.

How Can YOU Help?

Even though you are kids, you can help too! * **Wash Your Hands:** This is the BEST thing you can do to stay healthy from lots of germs! Use soap and water, and scrub like you're polishing a treasure. * **Eat Good Food:** Eating yummy fruits and vegetables makes your body strong and ready to fight off any germs. * **Get Enough Sleep:** When you sleep, your body rests and gets stronger. * **Listen to Grown-ups:** Your parents and teachers always know what's best to keep you safe and healthy. Did you know? Washing your hands properly with soap and water can remove millions of tiny germs! It's like magic!

Why is This News Important?

This news tells us that grown-ups are paying close attention. They are working hard and being very careful to protect everyone in DR Congo and beyond. It shows that when a problem comes, people come together to solve it. It’s a message of hope and teamwork! The goal is to stop the sickness from spreading and make sure everyone gets the help they need. The big helpers are doing a fantastic job, and they will keep working until everyone is safe and sound.

A Message of Hope

So, remember, my amazing African friends: there are kind and clever people working very hard to keep our world healthy. The people in DR Congo are strong, and they have many helpers. We can all do our part by staying healthy and being kind to each other. Keep smiling and keep washing those hands! See you next time!
summary: Big doctor teams and helpers from all over the world are working hard to stop a serious sickness called Ebola from spreading in our friends' country, DR Congo, and keep everyone safe.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows that grown-ups are paying close attention and working together to keep all people in Africa and around the world healthy and safe from serious sicknesses.

originalTitle: Toddlers among more than 50 schoolchildren kidnapped in Nigeria
title: Kind Hearts for Kids in Nigeria
content:
Hello, Amazing Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you important news from our wonderful continent of Africa. Today, we're talking about something that happened in a town called Mussa, far away in a country called Nigeria. Nigeria is a very big and beautiful country in West Africa, full of amazing people, animals, and stories!

What Happened in Mussa Town?

Sometimes, sad things happen. Recently, in Mussa town, some schoolchildren went missing. These were lovely boys and girls, just like you, who love to learn and play. Some of them were even tiny toddlers, very little kids, who should be playing with their toys and learning their ABCs. More than 50 children went missing from their school and their homes. This made their families and friends very, very sad and worried.

Sad News for Families

Imagine if your friend or brother or sister suddenly wasn't there. That’s how the families in Mussa town felt. They miss their children so much. Every grown-up and child in Mussa wants these children to come back home safely. It's a very difficult time for them, and our hearts go out to them. When something like this happens, it makes a whole town feel very sad and worried. We call this "devastated." It means everyone is deeply upset. Did you know? Nigeria is the most populated country in Africa! It has over 200 million people, which is like having a super big, friendly family!

Who Are Our Friends in Nigeria?

Nigeria is famous for its lively music, delicious food, and friendly people. Many children there go to school just like you, learning math, reading, and playing games. They have dreams of becoming doctors, teachers, or even news reporters! Education is super important, and schools are special places where children can grow and learn safely.

Helping Hands Everywhere

When something like this happens, many grown-ups jump into action to help. The police in Nigeria are working very hard. They are looking everywhere, asking questions, and trying to find clues. Think of them like super detectives, always on the case! Soldiers are also helping to look for the missing children, working day and night. Did you know? Borno State, where Mussa town is, is in the northeastern part of Nigeria. It's known for its rich cultural heritage and beautiful landscapes!

Grown-Ups Are Working Hard

It’s not just the police and soldiers. People in Mussa town and other towns nearby are helping too. They are talking to each other, sharing information, and praying for the children to come back. When everyone works together, it’s like a super team trying to solve a puzzle. Their main goal is to bring every single child home, safe and sound, back to their loving families.

What Does "Responsibility" Mean?

Our news tells us that no group has yet said "We did this." In grown-up words, this means no one has "claimed responsibility." It's like if something goes missing in your classroom, and no one says "I know where it is" or "I took it by mistake." So, the grown-ups don't know exactly who took the children, but they are still looking very hard for the children themselves. Did you know? The capital city of Nigeria is Abuja, but the biggest city is Lagos, which is a very busy and exciting place near the ocean!

A Community That Cares

This sad event has made many people around the world think about the children of Mussa town. People are sending messages of hope and support. It shows how we are all connected, like one big global family. When one part of our family is hurting, other parts want to help and offer comfort.

Why This Matters To All Of Us

It is important for all children everywhere to feel safe, especially at school and at home. Schools should be happy places where you feel secure and can focus on learning and making friends. When children are taken away, it reminds us how important it is for grown-ups to protect children and make sure they are safe. This is why so many grown-ups are working tirelessly to bring the Mussa children home. Did you know? In many parts of Africa, children walk long distances to go to school because they are so excited to learn! Education is a treasure.

A Message of Hope

We want to send lots of love and good thoughts to the children of Mussa town and their families. We hope they will be reunited very, very soon. Remember, there are many kind people in the world, and many grown-ups are trying their best to make things better. Let's keep hoping and sending our good wishes for the safe return of all the missing children. Did you know? Children in Africa often help their families with chores, like fetching water or helping with cooking. They are very responsible!

Let's Be Kind

Even though we might live far away, we can still show we care. We can be kind to our friends, listen to our teachers, and always try to make our own communities safe and happy places. Every little act of kindness makes the world a better place, one smile at a time. The world is thinking of our friends in Mussa.
summary: Many people are working hard to bring children who went missing from Mussa town in Nigeria back home safely to their families.
whyMatters: It is very important that all children feel safe at school and at home, and that grown-ups protect them, so everyone is helping to find the missing kids.

originalTitle: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
title: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
content: President Donald Trump said the operation eliminated "the most active terrorist in the world", Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

originalTitle: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC a global health emergency
title: Brave Doctors Help Fight Sickness in Africa!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your friendly reporter, coming to you live from our amazing continent, Africa! We have some important news to share today, but don't worry, we'll explain everything in a super simple way. Sometimes, grown-ups talk about big words, but we're going to make sure you understand every single thing. Our doctors and helpers are always working super hard to keep everyone safe and healthy, and that's what we'll talk about today.

What is Happening?

Right now, in a country called the Democratic Republic of the Congo, or DRC for short, some people have gotten sick. This sickness is called Ebola. It's a special kind of Ebola, like how there are different kinds of apples, but all are apples! This kind is called Bundibugyo. Sadly, some people have gotten very, very sick, and some have even died. When many people get sick in one place, we call it an 'outbreak'. But don't worry, many clever people are helping!

What is Ebola?

So, what exactly is Ebola? Imagine a really, really bad cold, but much, much worse. It's caused by a tiny, tiny germ, too small to see, that gets inside people's bodies. It makes them feel very hot, gives them a tummy ache, and makes them feel super tired. Doctors call this tiny germ a 'virus'. It's not like a normal cold that you catch when someone sneezes near you. Ebola doesn't spread through the air, so you won't catch it just by being near someone who has it. Ebola usually spreads when you touch someone who is very, very sick with it, or if you touch their blood or sweat. That's why doctors wear special suits, like brave superheroes, to protect themselves when they help sick people. It's very important to keep clean and wash your hands often to stay healthy from all kinds of germs! **Did you know?** The word "Ebola" comes from a river in Africa, near where this sickness was first found many years ago!

Where is this Happening?

The Democratic Republic of the Congo, or DRC, is a very big country in Central Africa. It has beautiful forests and many amazing animals! It's quite far from many other parts of Africa, and very, very far from other continents. Imagine a really long car ride or a long airplane trip! So, this problem is mostly happening in specific parts of this country right now, not everywhere. **Did you know?** The DRC is home to mountain gorillas, which are some of the rarest and biggest monkeys in the whole world!

What Does "Global Health Emergency" Mean?

Sometimes, when a sickness is a really big problem, a special group of grown-ups steps in to help. This important group is called the WHO. WHO stands for World Health Organization. Think of them as the world's biggest team of doctors and health helpers! When the WHO says something is a 'global health emergency,' it means this sickness needs extra attention and help from everywhere. It's like when your teacher says, 'Everyone, this is super important!' It means we all need to work together to stop the sickness and keep people safe. It's not just a problem for the DRC, but for everyone around the world to think about and help with. **Did you know?** The WHO helps many countries around the world with different health problems, like giving out special medicines called vaccines to protect against other sicknesses!

How Do We Stay Safe from Ebola?

The most important thing is that doctors and health workers know how to stop Ebola. It's not easy, but they are very smart and brave! They teach people to wash their hands very, very well, using lots of soap and clean water. They also teach people not to touch anyone who is sick, especially if they have Ebola. If someone is sick, they go to a special safe place called a treatment center, where doctors and nurses can take care of them without getting sick themselves. Sometimes, scientists can make special medicine called a vaccine. A vaccine is like a tiny shield that goes into your body and teaches it how to fight off germs, so you don't get sick. Doctors are using these special vaccines to help protect people in the DRC from getting Ebola. They are also looking for new medicines to make sick people feel better quickly. **Did you know?** Handwashing with soap and water is one of the best ways to stop all sorts of germs from spreading, not just Ebola!

Heroes in Action!

There are so many heroes working right now in the DRC. Doctors, nurses, and health workers are wearing their special protective suits, which look a bit like astronaut outfits. They are helping sick people feel better and making sure the sickness doesn't spread to more people. They are teaching families about hygiene, which means keeping clean and healthy, like washing hands and keeping homes tidy. Other helpers are bringing food, clean water, and special supplies to the areas where people are sick. It's a big team effort, with people from all over the world coming together to help our friends in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. We are so proud of these brave men and women! They are truly making a difference.

Why Should We Care?

Even if the DRC is far away from where you live, it's important to know about what's happening. When people in one part of the world are having a hard time, it's good for all of us to learn and understand. It helps us remember to be kind and think about others. And it reminds us that doctors and scientists are always working to make our world a healthier place for everyone, no matter where they live. We are all part of one big global family.

Stay Curious and Kind!

So that's the news about Ebola in the DRC. Remember, our doctors and helpers are doing an amazing job. They are working hard every day to make things better. We hope everyone there gets well soon. Keep washing your hands, stay healthy, and keep being kind to each other! See you next time for more news from Africa!
summary: Brave doctors and helpers are working very hard in the Democratic Republic of the Congo to stop a sickness called Ebola from spreading and to help people get well.
whyMatters: This is important because when people in one place get sick, everyone needs to work together to help them and keep everyone else safe and healthy, no matter where they live.

originalTitle: Man drives car into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight
title: Important News: Stay Safe Near Roads!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends all across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here, ready to share an important story with you today. We always talk about good things happening in our communities, but sometimes we also learn about things that remind us to be careful and kind. This story comes from a country very, very far away from us, called Italy. Can you say "Italy"? It's a place with lovely food like pizza and pasta!

What Happened Far Away?

One sunny day in Italy, something unexpected happened. A car was driving, but it went onto a sidewalk where people were walking. A sidewalk is that special path next to the road, just for people who are walking on their feet. These people are called "pedestrians." Can you say "pedestrians"? It just means someone who is walking! Sadly, because the car went onto the sidewalk, some people got hurt. It was a bit of a scary moment for everyone there. But guess what? Even when bad things happen, there are always good people ready to help. And that's a very important part of our story today.

Who Got Hurt?

In this story, eight people got hurt. That means eight people got some ouchies. Imagine getting a scraped knee when you fall, or a little bump on your head. That's what "injured" means – you got a little hurt. Four of these people had bigger ouchies, meaning they were a bit more seriously hurt. But don't worry too much, my friends. When people get hurt, especially in accidents, doctors and nurses are always there to help them feel better. They are like real-life superheroes who fix boo-boos and make sure everyone is okay. So, these people who were hurt went to the hospital to get special help. They were looked after very well.

Brave Helpers!

This is my favourite part of the story! When the car went onto the sidewalk, other people who were walking or nearby saw what was happening. These people are called "passers-by." They didn't just stand there; they quickly knew they had to help. They were very brave! These amazing helpers ran towards the car. They wanted to make sure no one else got hurt and to stop the car. And they did it! They were able to stop the man who was driving the car. Isn't that incredible? They showed so much courage and care for others. They were true community heroes. Did you know? Even small acts of kindness can make a big difference! These brave people saw someone needed help, and they acted. We can all be helpers in our own ways, too!

Why Are Roads Important?

Roads are super important, aren't they? They help us get from one place to another. Cars, buses, bicycles, and sometimes even motorbikes travel on roads. Roads help us go to school, visit our grandmas, or go to the market. But because many things move on roads, we must always be very, very careful. Roads have special rules to keep everyone safe. Drivers have to follow traffic lights and speed limits. And us, as pedestrians, also have very important rules to follow when we are near roads. Being safe on the road is a job for everyone! Did you know? The first roads were just paths made by people walking or animals! Now, we have big, strong roads made of tar and concrete.

Road Safety Rules for Kids!

Since we are talking about roads, let's remember some super important road safety rules. These rules help keep us safe whether we are in Italy, or right here in our wonderful African countries. 1. **Always hold a grown-up's hand:** When you are walking near or crossing a road, make sure you hold a trusted adult's hand very tightly. 2. **Look Left, Right, Left:** Before you cross any road, stop at the edge. Look to your left, then to your right, and then to your left again. Make sure no cars are coming. Only cross when the road is completely clear. 3. **Use Zebra Crossings:** Have you seen those white stripes on the road that look like a zebra? Those are called zebra crossings! They are special places for people to cross the road safely. Always use them if they are there. 4. **Don't Play Near Roads:** Roads are for driving and walking, not for playing. Keep your games far away from the road to stay safe. 5. **Be Seen:** If you are walking when it's getting dark, try to wear bright colours so drivers can see you easily. These rules are like magic spells that keep us safe!

Did You Know? About Cars!

Cars are amazing machines, aren't they? They take us on adventures and help grown-ups get to work. But cars are also powerful, and that's why drivers must be very careful. Did you know? Traffic lights have three colours: red, yellow, and green. Red means "stop," yellow means "get ready," and green means "go." These lights help keep cars and people safe at busy crossings. Only special people called "traffic police" can direct traffic without lights! Drivers go to school to learn how to drive safely and they get a special paper called a "driver's license." This shows they know all the rules. So, remember, the people driving cars are supposed to be very careful and follow all the rules to protect everyone.

Helpful People Everywhere!

In our story from Italy, we learned about the brave passers-by who helped. But there are many other helpful people in our world! We have police officers who make sure everyone follows the rules and stays safe. They also help if there's an emergency. We have doctors and nurses who work in hospitals and clinics. They help us when we are sick or when we get hurt. They have special medicines and tools to make us feel better. We are so lucky to have these wonderful people who care for us. Did you know? If you ever see someone in trouble or something dangerous, the best thing to do is *immediately tell a grown-up you trust*. It could be your mum, dad, teacher, or any other kind adult. They know what to do to help!

What Can We Learn?

So, what can we take away from this story, even though it happened far, far away? * **Safety First:** Always remember to be super careful when you are near roads. Your safety is very important! * **Be a Helper:** Just like those brave people in Italy, we can all be helpers in our community. If you see someone who needs help, and you can safely help (or get an adult to help), do it! * **Kindness Matters:** Sometimes accidents happen, and it's important to be kind and understanding to everyone involved. * **Trust Your Grown-Ups:** Listen to your parents, teachers, and other grown-ups. They teach you important rules like road safety to keep you happy and healthy.

A Message From Africa!

My dear friends, even though this story happened very far away, the lessons are for all of us, no matter where we live. Let's all promise to be safe when we are walking or playing, and let's always be ready to help each other. We are a big, wonderful family here in Africa and across the world. Let's keep looking out for each other and making our communities safe and happy places for everyone! Thank you for listening to our news report today. See you next time! Stay safe and keep smiling!
summary: In a faraway country called Italy, some people got hurt when a car went onto the sidewalk, but brave helpers quickly stepped in to make sure everyone was safe, reminding us all to be careful near roads and to always help each other.
whyMatters: This story helps us remember to always be very careful when walking near roads, to follow safety rules, and to know that there are always kind people who will help when things go wrong.
